old_claim.note: Military Map Rooms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because military_map_rooms is now scoped to the Depot de la Guerre founded in 1688, later than the 1614 military_engineer_corps node.

source_shape.source_locator: Ministere de la Culture glossary entry dates the Depot de la Guerre to 1688 and describes it as a bureau producing and conserving military maps.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places the corrected Depot node decades after the current military_engineer_corps date.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A 1688 institution cannot be a prerequisite for a 1614 engineer-corps node, and the source describes the Depot as a later archive/cartography bureau rather than the cause of earlier professional engineers.

ontology_before: Military Engineer Corps depended directly on the generic Military Map Rooms node through an unsourced expert-inference edge.
ontology_after: Military Engineer Corps no longer depends on the later Depot de la Guerre node; its remaining direct prerequisites cover fortification schooling and technical drawing.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct military_engineer_corps -> military_map_rooms edge removed.
would_reject_if: The military_engineer_corps node were redated to a post-1688 French Depot-specific corps.
The military_map_rooms node were split and an earlier source-backed military-map archive became available.
A source showed the 1614 engineer-corps claim actually depends on the 1688 Depot rather than earlier military engineering institutions.
short_reason: The corrected Depot node is later than the engineer-corps node, so the direct prerequisite was time-reversed.
